content.subtitle: All modal verbs
items: stay the same for every subject — there is no -s in the third person: She **can** drive. / He **should** call.
are always followed by a bare infinitive (without *to*): You **must wear** a helmet.
have no infinitive form of their own.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: To form a question, invert the modal and the subject: **Should** we leave now?
Modals can also be used in passive structures: The form **must be signed** by a parent.

rows.use: Ability in the present or in general
rows.example: My little sister **can** already ride a bike.
When he was young, he **could** run really fast.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: Use **be able to** when you need a tense that *can* does not have: It's great to **be able to** work from home.
Future: After more practice, you'll **be able to** speak Spanish fluently.
Present perfect: Have you **been able to** reach her on the phone?

rows.example: **May** I open the window? / **Could** I leave a few minutes early?
You **can** use the printer whenever you like.
You really **should** / **ought to** get more sleep if you're tired all the time.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: **May** sounds more formal and polite than **could**, and **could** is more polite than **can**.

sections.title: A: Complete with can, could or be able to
sections.instructions: Write the correct form in each gap.

items.sentence: I enjoy swimming, but I ...... dive very well yet.
When my dad was young, he ...... climb trees easily.
After a few more lessons, she'll ...... ride a motorbike safely.
They haven't ...... contact the hotel yet.
...... you speak English when you were ten?
If we save enough money, we'll ...... buy a better computer.
I'm sorry, I ...... meet you after school today.
He wasn't ...... open the door because he had lost the key.

sections.title: B: Permission (choose the correct modal)
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: **Can / May** I speak to the manager, please?
You **can / could** use my charger if your phone battery is low.
**Could / Can** you help me carry this box, please?
Visitors **may / could** enter the museum after 10 a.m.
**May / Can** I have another copy of the form?
You **couldn't / can't** swim here. It's dangerous.
Excuse me, **could / may** you show me the way to the library?
No, you **may not / could not** take photographs inside the gallery.

sections.title: C: Advice (should / ought to)
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with should, shouldn't, ought to or ought not to.

items.sentence: It's raining. You ...... take an umbrella.
We ...... leave our bags unattended at the station.
If she wants to improve, she ...... practise every day.
You ...... spend all afternoon looking at a screen.
What ...... we do if the train is cancelled?
You ...... ask your parents before you go out.
Drivers ...... use their phones while driving.
We ...... check the address before we leave.

sections.title: D: Rewrite using a modal
sections.instructions: Rewrite each sentence using the modal in brackets.

items.sentence:
Is it possible for you to send me the file today? (can)
...... you send me the file today?

It is not a good idea to eat too quickly. (shouldn't)
You ...... eat too quickly.

I had the ability to play chess when I was six. (could)
I ...... play chess when I was six.

Is it all right if I open the door? (may)
...... I open the door?

It is not a good idea to leave your bike unlocked. (shouldn't)
You ...... leave your bike unlocked.

They have managed to repair the machine. (been able to)
They have ...... repair the machine.

sections.title: E: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using a modal verb so that it means the same as the first.

items.sentence:
It's a bad idea to walk home alone late at night.
You ...... home alone late at night.

Is it OK if I borrow your bike?
...... your bike?

When I was nine, I had the ability to cook simple meals.
When I was nine, I ...... .

Soon they will manage to finish the bridge.
Soon they will ...... finish the bridge.

Do you think I should apologise to her?
Do you think I ...... to her?

Please don't enter this room without permission.
You ...... this room without permission.
'I feel really nervous about the interview.' 'You ...... so much. Just be yourself.'
When I was younger, I ...... draw very realistic animals.
'...... I use your ruler?' 'Yes, of course.'
You ...... take a coat because it's cold outside.
By the end of the course, you ...... design your own website.
People ...... talk loudly in the library.

sections.title: G: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake with a modal. Write the correct sentence.

items.sentence: He cans ride a horse.
You should to call your grandmother.
May I to ask a question?
We didn't could find the address.
She will can join us next week.
items.answer: She will be able to join us next week.

items.sentence: They ought not arrive so late.
items.answer: They ought not to arrive so late.

sections.title: H: Mixed practice
sections.instructions: Complete each gap with one suitable modal expression.

items.sentence: Excuse me, ...... I sit next to you?
You ...... drink more water when it's hot.
I ...... tie my shoes until I was five.
With more practice, you'll ...... play this song perfectly.
...... you possibly speak more slowly, please?
We ...... book the tickets before they sell out.
Visitors ...... touch the paintings in the museum.
My cousin ...... speak Italian quite well now.

sections.title: I: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using a maximum of four words, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence.

items.sentence:
I knew how to swim at the age of six.
I ...... at the age of six.

It's against the rules to take photos here.
You ...... photos here.

The dentist told me to eat fewer sweets.
The dentist said I ...... fewer sweets.

She managed to answer all the questions correctly.
She ...... all the questions correctly.

Is it OK to use dictionaries in the test?
...... dictionaries in the test?

My coach says I should train more often.
My coach says I ...... more often.
